task.title: Creating a NetworkManager dispatcher script that runs dhclient exit hooks

li.p: The dhclient exit hooks are stored in the /etc/dhcp/dhclient-exit-hooks.d/ directory.

context.p: When a DHCP server assigns or updates an IPv4 address, NetworkManager can run a dispatcher script stored in the /etc/dhcp/dhclient-exit-hooks.d/ directory. This dispatcher script can then, for example, run dhclient exit hooks.

step.cmd: Create the /etc/NetworkManager/dispatcher.d/12-dhclient-down file with the following content:

info.codeblock:
#!/bin/bash # Run dhclient.exit-hooks.d scripts
if [ -n "$DHCP4_DHCP_LEASE_TIME" ] ; then if [ "$2" = "dhcp4-change" ] || [ "$2" = "up" ] ; then if [ -d /etc/dhcp/dhclient-exit-hooks.d ] ; then for f in /etc/dhcp/dhclient-exit-hooks.d/*.sh ; do if [ -x "${f}" ]; then . "${f}" fi done fi fi fi

step.cmd: Set the root user as owner of the file:
Set the permissions so that only the root user can execute it:
